title: Industrial Protocol Manipulation Sandbox
description: Simulates an attacker moving from a corporate network to an isolated OT network by leveraging weak EWS credentials, followed by unauthenticated Modbus TCP manipulation to halt a critical process.

levels.title: Initial Access & Recon
levels.content:
You are on the corporate network (`10.10.10.50`). A poorly configured Engineering Workstation (`192.168.20.20`) acts as a bridge to the OT network. Compromise the EWS using default credentials (`operator` / `operator123`). From there, perform internal reconnaissance. 

What is the IP address of the active PLC on the control subnet?
levels.solution: SSH into `operator@192.168.20.20`. Use `ping` or `nmap` on the `192.168.20.0/24` subnet to discover the PLC at `192.168.20.10`.

levels.title: Process Sabotage (Modbus Manipulation)
levels.content:
The PLC at `192.168.20.10` controls a cooling pump mapped to Modbus Holding Register 0 (Address 40001). During normal operation, this register holds the value `1`. 

Using the `modbus` CLI tool pre-installed on the EWS, write the value `0` to this register to halt the cooling pump. Once the value is zeroed, a safety mechanism will dump a diagnostic log to `/var/log/safety_override.txt` on the EWS containing your confirmation.
levels.solution: Run `modbus write 192.168.20.10 40001 0` from the EWS terminal. Read the generated file to get the confirmation code.

hints.title: Modbus CLI Syntax
hints.content: Use the command `modbus write <IP> <Register> <Value>` to alter the controller's memory.
